title: Free Demo — 10 Sample Plumber Questions
description: Try 10 questions from across all 306A exam topics. No signup required.

questions.question: What is the minimum slope (grade) for a 3-inch or smaller horizontal drain pipe?
questions.explanation: Horizontal drain pipes 3 inches and smaller require a minimum slope of 1/4 inch per foot (1:48) to ensure proper flow and self-cleaning velocity.

questions.question: Which type of backflow preventer provides the highest level of protection and is required for severe hazard cross-connections?
questions.explanation: The Reduced Pressure Backflow Preventer (RPBP / RP) provides the highest level of protection. It has two independently operating check valves with a pressure differential relief valve between them, and is required for severe (health) hazard cross-connections.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a plumbing trap?
options: A) To regulate water pressure in the system
B) To prevent sewer gases from entering the building
C) To filter debris from wastewater
D) To increase drainage flow velocity
questions.explanation: A plumbing trap maintains a water seal (minimum 50mm / 2 inches) that prevents sewer gases, odours, and vermin from entering the building through the drainage openings.

questions.question: What safety device is required on every storage-type water heater?
options: B) Temperature and Pressure (T&P) relief valve
questions.explanation: Every storage-type water heater must have a Temperature and Pressure (T&P) relief valve to prevent dangerous pressure buildup. The discharge pipe must terminate no more than 150mm (6 inches) above the floor and must not be threaded at the end.

questions.question: A vent terminal through a roof must extend a minimum of how far above the roof surface?
questions.explanation: A vent terminal must extend at least 150 mm (6 inches) above the roof surface. However, in areas subject to snow, the vent must extend higher — typically 600 mm (24 inches) above the anticipated snow level.

questions.question: When pressure testing a new DWV system with air, what is the minimum test pressure and hold time?
questions.explanation: A DWV air test requires a minimum of 35 kPa (approximately 5 psi) held for at least 15 minutes with no drop in pressure. Water testing is an alternative at the full height of the stack.

questions.question: What method is used to size natural gas piping in a building?
options: A) Water supply fixture unit method
B) Longest run method using total BTU/h demand
C) Pipe diameter equals drain size method
D) Velocity method based on water equivalents
questions.explanation: Gas piping is sized using the longest run method: determine the total BTU/h (input) demand of all appliances, measure the longest pipe run from the meter to the farthest appliance, then use the sizing tables in CSA B149.1 to select the correct pipe diameter.

questions.question: Where should the circulating pump typically be installed in a closed hydronic heating system?
options: A) On the return line, farthest from the boiler
B) On the supply line, immediately after the boiler
C) On the return line, near the boiler (pumping away from expansion tank)
D) On the supply line, after the expansion tank connection
questions.explanation: The circulator pump is typically installed on the return line near the boiler, pumping away from the point of no pressure change (expansion tank connection). This ensures the pump adds pressure to the system rather than creating negative pressure that could cause air ingestion.

questions.question: What is the minimum horizontal separation distance between a drilled well and a septic tank in Ontario?
questions.explanation: In Ontario, the minimum horizontal separation between a drilled well and a septic tank is 15 metres (approximately 50 feet). Greater distances are required from the leaching bed / tile field — typically 30 metres.

questions.question: A water closet (toilet) is rated at how many fixture units for drainage sizing?
questions.explanation: A water closet (toilet) is rated at 4 fixture units (FU) for drainage sizing purposes. This value is used when calculating the total fixture unit load on drain pipes and determining required pipe sizes.
